meta_description: Learn about ABRANB: who we are and how we work.

body_segments.value: spread across Brazil in 14 states through its members and local/regional groups. We put
non-binarity on the agenda broadly across many themes
according to members’ availability and willingness, working
issues that matter for non-binary people’s rights

. We seek and have built ties with state public defenders’ offices, civil registries, and clinics focused on care for our community, as well as the Red No Binarie Abya Yala network, coordinating across Latin America.
In 2021 ABRANB was founded on principles of horizontality and autonomy
It shared educational materials about our community intensively with the Inter-American Commission on Human Rights
It expanded across as many Brazilian states as possible and organized into thematic hubs (Health, Law, etc.) and regional groups
It consolidated data on how trans health clinics operate in several cities, identifying barriers and model services
, a tool to track legal cases relevant to the non-binary population

mapa_title: Official non-binary recognition map by state
mapa_alt: Map of Brazil colored by non-binary recognition status in each state
principios_items: Independence/autonomy in relation to other organizations (collectives, NGOs, unions, parties, mandates, companies)
Fair distribution of tasks and activities, and collective commitment
Fair division of speaking time for everyone
No one speaks on behalf of the articulation without prior consultation with our collective
No lawsuits or legal measures against another member for personal fights or ego disputes
